reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The variable 'c' is used uninitialized in the first call to isalpha if fgetc returns EOF immediately; also, isalpha expects unsigned char or EOF, but passing potentially negative char values can cause undefined behavior. However, the primary issue here is logic: it skips non-alphabetic characters correctly, but the do-while loop may read past the word boundary incorrectly when file ends without trailing non-alpha. But more critically, missing #include <ctype.h> causes implicit declaration of isalpha/tolower, leading to compilation error.]
